feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Custom Account Modifier (Z) Not Appearing in Automatic Account Determination – SAP S/4HANA GROW
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Grow) and facing an issue related to Automatic Account Determination.

Here is my scenario:

  • I created a custom movement type (copy of 201).

  • In the movement type configuration, I changed the Account Grouping (General Modification) from standard value VBR to a custom value Z03.

  • The movement type configuration was saved successfully.

However, when I go to:

SSCUI 100297 – Configure Automatic Postings
Transaction Key: GBB

I cannot find Z03 in the “General Modification” list.
Only standard values such as VBR, AUF, VBO, etc., are available.

My questions:

  1. Is it technically possible to use a custom Account Modifier (Z**) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

  2. Is there any way to expose custom account modifiers in Automatic Account Determination?

  3. Or is this restricted by SAP’s Cloud configuration framework?

I understand that Public Cloud follows clean core principles, but I would like to confirm whether this is a system limitation or if there is an additional configuration step required.

Any insights or best practice recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Custom General Modification (Z03) Not Available in GBB – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Grow) and facing an issue related to Automatic Account Determination.

Here is my scenario:

I created a custom movement type (copy of 201).

In the movement type configuration, I changed the Account Grouping (General Modification) from standard value VBR to a custom value Z03.

The movement type configuration was saved successfully.

However, when I go to:

SSCUI 100297 – Configure Automatic Postings
Transaction Key: GBB

I cannot find Z03 in the “General Modification” list.
Only standard values such as VBR, AUF, VBO, etc., are available.

My questions:

Is it technically possible to use a custom Account Modifier (Z**) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Is there any way to expose custom account modifiers in Automatic Account Determination?

Or is this restricted by SAP’s Cloud configuration framework?

I understand that Public Cloud follows clean core principles, but I would like to confirm whether this is a system limitation or if there is an additional configuration step required.

Any insights or best practice recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / Uninterupted Workflow for Parked Invoice as Complete
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
i have a question regarding the Configuration of the Workflow for Parked Invoice as Complete (WS00800303).
I have it setup as follows:
1. One Employee creates the Supplier invoice and parks it.
2. The second employee has to check the invoice and make changes if necessary.
3. The third employee makes a final check and posts the invoice

The issue for me is, that currently if the employee in step 2 or step 3 makes a change to the supplier invoice and parks it again, the workflow gets canceled and then restarts from the beginning. 
My question is, is there a way to configure the workflow in a way that the change to the invoice doesnt restart the complete workflow and it just goes to the next employee ? 

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: Unable to add New EWM warehouse number to New Business partner in "Set warehouse Address"
entry.content:
I am unable to add the new EWM warehouse number to the new Business Partner in the “Set Warehouse Address” configuration.

I have created a new plant and a new EWM warehouse, and now I need to maintain the default party entities. However, when I try to add them, the system does not allow the entry and displays the error shown below.

Kindly assist in resolving this issue.

entry.title: Create Physical Inventory Documents
entry.content:
Are there any latest hot fixes or release notes for the app  - Create Physical Inventory Documents?

We are seeing too many filters and columns in app. This has confused user at many levels. For ex. Why are zero quantity batches displaying in app? Do we need to apply every time filter for current book quantity greater than zero? Earlier all those filters were not required to apply while creating physical inventory documents. Please let me know.

entry.title: Deficit of Stck w.vend.unr./rev X PC XXX IN PO Subcontracting SAP S/4HANA CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
Good day experts.

I receive the following message when posting a Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document.

1. Create a subcontract order for 10 pieces.

2.Without performing the outbound delivery of the components, and going directly to Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document, the system allowed me to record an entry for 6 pieces, but it did not allow me to do so for the rest and showed me the message I attached.

3.Besides skipping outbound delivery, I also didn't have my components in stock.

Why does the system allow this? Is it a CBC setting? As you can see from the purchase order, the info record, and my material settings, I don't have it configured to allow this.

Thank you for your comments.

entry.title: Project Budget Report - Budget (AVC) Value
entry.content:
Hi!

We are building a Project Budget Report Custom Reporting App in Public Cloud.

Although there is a standard app which provides all the reporting details, but it is applicable only for services and not for materials.
To provide reporting details of materials + services, we have approached our client to build a custom app.

Currently the challenge we are facing is fetching the Budget (AVC) value.
I have checked CDS views related to WBS elements, but could not find any appropriate CDS where the Total budget for a WBS Element can be fetched.

As per the process, a project is created, a WBS is assigned under it and initial budget is uploaded.

The fields we are displaying in output is similar the standard app.

1. Actuals
2. Commitments
3. Withheld Commitments (displays values if document is under approval)
4. Available Budget
5. Total Budget

We have already implemented the logic to display the values in the 1st three fields based on the values of the documents posted (PR - PO - GR) and it is working fine

If we can fetch this total budget value, then subsequently we can apply the calculations to derive the Available Budget = (Total Budget - (Commitments+Witheld Commitments+Actuals) for a WBS Element. That way our report can be completed.

Can anyone help me the resolve this. We are at 80% completion for this development.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Product Division in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Currently customer has 1 sales org. with 4 divisions. Products are segregated as per the division. Sales Org. (1000) having 4 different divisions (10, 20, 30, 40) for sales and 2 distribution channels (wholesale and Retail (newly added)). As per customer requirement, wholesale distribution channel cannot sell product across divisions in single sales order, but retail distribution channel must allow selling products across different divisions in a single sales order.

In Product Master if we maintain the division in basic data then same is extended to Sales org. as well applicable to all distribution channels. This will prevent selling products in Retail distribution channel when products from different divisions required in single sale order. If Division not maintained in the Product master, then system will not restrict selling products across the division in wholesale distribution channel. We have proposed creation of separate division (00-Common) used for Retail sales and maintenance of Product exclusion at sales area level. But customer is highlighting lot of data maintenance required for the exclusion. Any better suggestion will be helpful.

entry.title: Planning calendar customizing in public cloud
entry.content: Hello Community, is there any best practice for maintaining planning calendar in public cloud? Assuming a plant will be live on public cloud in the year 2026. From which year should this customizing be maintained?

entry.title: Commitments in Company Code
entry.content:
Good morning,

I am analyzing commitments when performing budget control for cost centers.

The other day, I had a question: when you create the purchase order, that is when the commitment is generated. That much is clear to me.

But what happens when you have the order approval in the workflow? I know that approval allows you to receive that order.

Let's imagine that it is rejected. We would no longer be able to receive the order, and from what I have seen, that commitment is not removed from the reports.

I also tried rejecting that order to see if it would be removed from the report, but it wasn't.

The only way I have found to remove it would be to mark the order position for deletion, in which case it would be removed.

I would like to know if there is a more automatic way to remove the commitment from the report once the order approval has been rejected.

Best regards.

Juan Antonio Moyano

entry.title: Manage Purchase Orders
entry.content:
Good morning,

I have placed a purchase order where I have added the WBS Element and made the advance payment request and payment for that order.

Due to circumstances, I had to change the WBS for the order, and when I went to edit it, the field could not be modified (I understood that it was not allowed because of the purchase order, since it has the advance payment linked to the order).

So, the option I thought of was to cancel the advance payment and the request, and this allowed me to modify the WBS in the purchase order.

I put the question to the gurus: is there any other way to edit that purchase order without having to cancel the payment and the advance request?

Best regards

Juan Antonio Moyano

entry.title: Cost center MIS not available in system; manual Excel reporting delays month-end closure.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently working on Management Reporting requirements in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Finance).

Our business has requested an MIS report in a format similar to Financial Statement Version (FSV), however with additional Cost Center wise grouping and reporting.

Current Understanding

As per standard system behavior, we observe that:

  • FSV configuration allows grouping only based on G/L Accounts under Nodes/Subnodes

  • Cost Center is a Controlling dimension and does not appear as a grouping element within FSV

Business Requirement

The business expects:

  • P&L / Expense reporting in an FSV-like structure

  • Breakup and drilldown by Cost Center (department wise MIS reporting)

  • Possibly future extension towards Product/SKU profitability reporting

Support Required from SAP

We request your guidance on the following:

  1. Is there any standard capability in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to enable Cost Center based grouping within an FSV structure?

  2. If not, what is the recommended best practice approach to deliver such MIS reporting?

    • Using standard analytical apps

    • SAC integration

    • Group Reporting / Management reporting framework

  3. Are there any SAP delivered CDS Views, Key User Extensibility options, or scope items that support this requirement?

  4. Can SAP suggest an end-to-end solution approach for Cost Center wise MIS reporting aligned with financial statement presentation?

We would appreciate your support and recommendations so that we can align the solution with SAP best practices and avoid non-supported customization.

Looking forward to your response.

entry.title: How to Set Up and Use the Capacity Scheduling Board in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
How can the Capacity Scheduling Board app be properly set up and used in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

What are the prerequisites for orders to be displayed, and which configuration settings must be checked?

entry.title: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content:
Hello,

We need to change the declarant details, such as name and ID, for VAT reporting generated from app Run Statutory Reports - the system is S4/HANA Public Cloud.

I know that this should be done via CBC, but I can't find the exact configuration item.

I'll much appreciate to get a short step-by-step configuration guide.

Thank you in advance!

 

Regards,

Plamen

Shelly Group

entry.title: How to make a field mandatory or warning trigger for miro
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We are using public cloud we wanted to make a field warning or error how do we proceed it in this scenario .

eg:-in miro in the headtab to make as warning message to enter ksef number :

entry.title: EWM Component staging not initiated
entry.content:
Hello experts.

We are implementing EWM in public cloud. In our system we stage using material independent control cycle using pick part strategy as shown:

Now, what we do is, after release of production orders, we go to functions -> WM Material Staging -> Execute. When we do that we see that the documents generated in the process posting change item app as shown below:

So our customizing system is working fine. Now we are in test system and what we are noticing is when we execute the staging, the quantity is not requested as shown in the simulation below:

In the log I see the message: Message 118 - Batch verification no required, although possible in WM (pick parts):

We have never seen this message in customizing system. Also one more thing I have noticed that in the component view 'Change Process Type' field is marked as 1 (Manually Changed):

In customizing system I have seen this field is blank.

Could you please help me understand what is the root cause of this issue? Why am I not able to execute the staging process? Any help/insight will be highly appreciated.

 

Thank you

Snigdhodeep

entry.title: GL Account not available as Preconditition for Market Segment Substitution
entry.content:
In S4HC Public, in the app Manage Substitution and Validation Rules (Market Segment Business context), we need to create a substitution whose precondition is a given GL Account. Unfortunately, this field is not available in the list (see capture 1 attached).

However, the GL account is available for the substitutions done through the custom logic BADI FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB (Market Segment Extension Fields Substitution).

 
 
Is it a mistake and SAP forgot to include GL account to be used in the app Manage Substitution?
We don't want to create custom field ZZ_glaccount and populate it using the before mentioned custom logic, as proposed for other issues as "clean core solution". Do we need to raise a note/incidence to SAP?
 
Thanks. Regards,
